asi.jest_config: react-native preset, babel-jest, no cache, node env

description: Add transformIgnorePatterns to skip transforming most node_modules (was transforming everything)

asi.hypothesis: transformIgnorePatterns:[] was forcing babel to transform ALL node_modules files; restricting to only needed packages saves ~5s
asi.key_insight: The empty transformIgnorePatterns array was the single biggest perf problem

description: Test-only babel config with single @react-native/babel-preset (removing redundant preset-env + preset-typescript)

asi.hypothesis: Removing redundant babel presets would speed up transforms
asi.rollback_reason: No improvement - within noise of previous 9.46s
asi.next_action_hint: Try maxWorkers tuning, or look at specific slow tests like plugin.test.ts

description: Set maxWorkers=50% to reduce worker overhead (13->6-7 workers). 9.5s->8.4s

asi.hypothesis: Too many workers cause overhead with 36 small test suites
asi.key_insight: Even 2-3 workers perform similarly because bottleneck is single slow suite (InterpolateColor ~8.7s)

description: Split into Jest projects (css vs core) to avoid loading full setup for CSS tests

asi.hypothesis: Separate Jest projects for CSS tests (no setup) and core tests (full setup) would be faster
asi.rollback_reason: Projects overhead made it slower: 9.1s vs 8.4s
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on reducing babel transform time or test execution time itself

description: Disable source maps in babel-jest transform to reduce overhead

asi.hypothesis: Source map generation adds babel overhead that's unnecessary for tests
asi.key_insight: Source maps are small overhead compared to actual babel transforms
